import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut current = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
        if has
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, -current)
          else
            result = list.push(result, current)
          end
          current = 0
          has = false
          neg = false
        end
      else if b == 45
        neg = true
        has = true
      else if b >= 48 and b <= 57
        current = current * 10 + (b - 48)
        has = true
      end
    when None
      i = i
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, -current)
    else
      result = list.push(result, current)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_target(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  list.index_of(xs, t)
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(line1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line2)
    let xs = parse_ints(line1)
    match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
    when Some(target)
      match find_target(xs, target)
      when Some(idx)
        println("index=#{idx}")
      when None
        println("not_found")
      end
    when None
      println("not_found")
    end
  when None
    println("not_found")
  end
when None
  println("not_found")
end
